message:
fix(database): DbPool mutex locks survive poisoned state via unwrap_or_else(into_inner)

All 11 Mutex::lock().unwrap() call sites in DbPool now use
unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) instead, recovering the inner
state when the mutex is poisoned (e.g. a panic during a prior
lock hold).  This prevents a single panicking task from permanently
bricking the entire connection pool — the pool continues operating
with whatever state the poisoned lock held.

This is the standard Rust pattern for mutexes protecting state that
is still structurally valid after a panic (counts, connection lists).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
